entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The crop Oryza sativa, which is targeted by the brown planthopper pest.

entities.name: BROWN PLANTHOPPER
entities.description: Nilaparvata lugens, a major pest of rice causing significant yield loss.

entities.name: BPH RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of rice cultivars to resist infestation by the brown planthopper.

entities.name: YIELD LOSS
entities.description: The reduction in crop productivity caused by BPH infestations.

entities.name: BIOTYPE 4
entities.description: A highly disastrous biotype of the brown planthopper found in the Indian sub-continent, also known as the South Asian Biotype.

entities.name: MAGIC PANEL
entities.description: A multi-parent advanced generation intercross panel consisting of 391 lines developed from eight indica founder parents.

entities.name: SNPS
entities.description: Single nucleotide polymorphisms used as markers for identifying trait associations.

entities.name: GENOME-WIDE ASSOCIATION ANALYSIS
entities.description: A method used to find consistent associations between markers and traits using single and multi-locus models.

entities.name: FARMPCU
entities.description: Fixed and random model circulating probability unification, used to identify significant marker-associations.

entities.name: NB-ARC DOMAIN-CONTAINING PROTEIN
entities.description: A gene product associated with BPH resistance.

entities.name: NHL REPEAT-CONTAINING PROTEIN
entities.description: A gene product associated with BPH resistance.

entities.name: LRR CONTAINING PROTEIN
entities.description: A gene product associated with BPH resistance.

entities.name: WRKY70
entities.description: A gene product associated with BPH resistance.

entities.name: CONTROLLED CONDITIONS
entities.description: The environment in which the MAGIC panel was phenotyped for two consecutive years.

entities.name: INDIAN SUB-CONTINENT
entities.description: The geographic region where BPH Biotype 4 is prevalent.
